Pelosi Lab
PublicationsAboutPeople

Publications

* Denotes equal contribution, + denotes undergraduate researcher.

Check out my Google Scholar page for updates.

In Review/In Revision

X. Pelosi, J.A., A. Yañez, L.N. Veldhusien, A.J. Dant, P.C. Northing, R.G.W. Bland, W.L. Testo, and K.M. Dlugosch. Testing fundamental hypotheses of colonization success in ferns. In review, submitted to Annals of Botany. Pre-print available: https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.04.16.719039

X. Pteridophyte Phylogeny Group II (Organized by J.H. Nitta, M. Hassler, H. Schneider, C. Rothfels, and E. Schuettpelz. With contributions from 155 authors including J.A. Pelosi [90 of 155]). An update on the Pteridophyte Phylogeny Group classification for extant ferns and lycophytes: PPG II. In revision at Taxon.

Published

2026

20. MacGuigan, D.J., H.M. Waterman, J.A. Pelosi, N.J.C. Backenstose, S.L. Chang, M.L. Tan, F. Almeida-Silva, and T.J. Krabbenhoft. In press at Systematic Biology. Pre-print available: https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.09.19.677351

19. Pelosi, J.A., T.R. Curry+, A.R. Pearse+, M.C. Smith, and K.M. Dlugosch. Near chromosome-level genome assembly of Neomuostima conspurcatalis gives insights into the evolution of moth genome architecture and fern-insect interactions. G3: Genes | Genomes | Genetics. 16(5): jkag065 (15 pages).

18. Kim, E.H.,+, K. Wheatly,+, J.A. Pelosi, L. Azevdeo-Schmidt, E.D. Currano, R.E. Dunn, J.L. Gill, J. Pitterman, and E.B. Sessa. How to survive a mass extinction: Lessons from fern gametophytes. The American Fern Journal. 116(2): 109-126.

2025

17. Pelosi, J.A., R. Davenport, L.-Y. Kuo, L.N. Gray, A.J. Dant, E.H. Kim+, F.-W. Li, K.M. Dlugosch, T.J. Krabbenhoft, W.B. Barbazuk, and E.B. Sessa. 2025. The genome of the vining fern Lygodium microphyllum highlights genomic and functional differences between life phases of an invasive plant. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 122(39): e2504773122. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2504773122

16. Smith, M., N. Sebesta, J.A. Pelosi, J. S. Glueckert , S. Enloe, and J. Aquino-Thomas. 2025. Efforts to control Lygodium microphyllum in Florida and developments in biology and management. CABI Reviews 20:1, 0040. https://doi.org/10.1079/cabireviews.2025.004

15. Northing, P.C., J.A. Pelosi, D.L. Venable, and K.M. Dlugosch Chromosome-scale reference genome of Pectocarya recurvata, a species with one of the smallest genome sizes in Boraginaceae. Applications in Plant Sciences. 13(3): e70008. (14 pages). https://doi.org/10.1002/aps3.70008

14. Pelosi, J.A., E.S. Sorojsrisom, W.B. Barbazuk, and E.B. Sessa. Population genomics of the gametophyte-only fern Vittaria appalachiana provides insights into clonal plant evolution. New Phytologist 246(1): 349-364. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.20433

2024

13. Reatini, B., J.A. Pelosi, F.A. Chang, Q. Jiang, M.T.W. McKibben, M.S. Barker, L.H. Rieseberg, and K.M. Dlugosch. Chromosome-scale reference genome and RAD-based genetic map of yellow starthistle (Centaurea solstitialis) reveal putative structural variation and QTL associated with invader traits. Genome Biology and Evolution 16(12): evae243 (17 pages). https://doi.org/10.1093/gbe/evae243

12. Pelosi, J.A., R. Davenport, W.B. Barbazuk, E.B. Sessa, and L.-Y. Kuo. An efficient and effective RNA extraction protocol for ferns. Applications in Plant Science 12(6): e11617 (8 pages). https://doi.org/10.1002/aps3.11617

  • Featured on the November-December 2024 edition of APPS

11. Pelosi, J.A., B.A. Zumwalde, W.L. Testo, E.H. Kim+, J.G. Burleigh, and E.B. Sessa. All tangled up: unraveling phylogenetics and reticulate evolution in the vining ferns, Lygodium (Lygodiaceae, Schizaeales). American Journal of Botany 111(9): e16389 (21 pages). https://doi.org/10.1002/ajb2.16389

2023

10. Pelosi, J.A., E.S. Sorojsrisom, B.A. Zumwalde, and E.B. Sessa. A genome size for the Appalachian gametophyte. The American Fern Journal 113(4): 257-262. https://doi.org/10.1640/0002-8444-113.4.257 * Featured on the December 2023 edition of AFJ.

9.\ Aros-Mualin, D., C. Flores-Galván, S. Páez, J.A. Pelosi, E.S. Sorojsrisom, N. Yawn, and J.E. Watkins. In situ observations of gametophytes of six fern species of Costa Rica. *The American Fern Journal *113(3): 170-190. https://doi.org/10.1640/0002-8444-113.3.170

7. Pelosi, J.A., W.B. Barbazuk, and E.B. Sessa. Life without a sporophyte: the origins and genomic consequences of asexual reproduction in a gametophyte-only fern. International Journal of Plant Sciences 184(6): 454-469. https://doi.org/10.1086/724824

6. Pelosi, J.A., B.A. Zumwalde, O. Hornych, K. Wheatly+, E.H. Kim+, and E.B. Sessa. Lygodium japonicum (Lygodiaceae) is represented by a tetraploid cytotype in Florida. The American Fern Journal 113(1):43-55. https://doi.org/10.1640/0002-8444-113.1.43

2022

5. Pelosi, J.A., E.H. Kim+, W.B. Barbazuk, and E.B. Sessa. 2022. Phylotranscriptomics illuminates the placement of whole genome duplications and gene retention in ferns. Frontiers in Plant Science 13:882441 https://doi.org/10.3389/fpls.2022.882441

2021

4. Pelosi, J.A., M.A. Bernal, T.J. Krabbenhoft, C. Prada, S. Galbo+, M.A. Coffroth, and H.R. Lasker. 2021. Fine-scale genomic, morphological, reproductive, and symbiont differences delimit the Caribbean octocorals Plexaura homomalla and P. kükenthali. Coral Reefs 41(3): 635-653. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00338-021-02175-x

3. Krabbenhoft, T.J., D.J. MacGuigan, N.C. Backenstose, H. Watterman, T. Lan, J.A. Pelosi, M. Tan, and S.R. Sandve. 2021. Chromosome-level genome assembly of Chinese sucker (Myxocyprinus asiaticus) reveals strongly conserved synteny following the Catostomid-specific whole genome duplication. Genome Biology and Evolution 13(9): evab190.

  • Featured on the September 2021 edition of GBE.

2. Pelosi, J.A. , K.M. Eaton, S. Mychajliw+, C. terHorst, and M.A. Coffroth. 2021. Thermally tolerant symbionts may explain Caribbean octocoral resilience to heat stress. Coral Reefs 40: 1113-1125. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00338-021-02116-8

1. Pelosi, J.A., and E.B. Sessa. 2021. From genomes to populations: a meta-analysis and review of fern population genetics. International Journal of Plant Sciences 182(5): 325-343 https://doi.org/10.1086/713442 ** Featured on the June 2021 edition of IJPS.

Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.
